market_rules:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) on February 5 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance. The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/NVDA/history, published under "Historical Prices."

# Nvidia Earnings on Feb. 25: What History Tells Us About Nvidia Stock's Post-Earnings-Release Moves
By [Beth McKenna] – Feb 17, 2026 at 6:00AM EST
## Key Points
*
Given Nvidia's great record of beating Wall Street’s earnings estimates, the probability seems very high that it will do so on Feb. 25.
*
An “earnings beat,” however, doesn’t necessarily mean the stock will rise following the release.
*

# Prediction: Nvidia Will Have a Difficult Time Living Up to Investors' Lofty Expectations on Feb. 25
By [Sean Williams] – Feb 16, 2026 at 7:06AM EST
## Key Points
*
Artificial intelligence (AI) is Wall Street's hottest trend, with the stock market's largest publicly traded company, Nvidia, leading the charge.
*
Demand for Nvidia's graphics processing units (GPUs) has been borderline insatiable, translating into superior pricing power and mouthwatering margins.
*
A myriad of factors suggest Nvidia's fiscal 2027 outlook won't satisfy investors.
*

Nvidia doesn't have to miss Wall Street's Q4 estimates for its share price to fall.

Many Nvidia ([NVDA] +1.74%) investors have probably circled Feb. 25, 2026, on their calendars. That's the date the giant chipmaker is scheduled to announce its fiscal 2025 fourth-quarter results. Nvidia plans to release its results following the market close and hold its quarterly conference call with analysts shortly afterward.

It's easy to predict that Nvidia's share price will surge after its Q4 update. The company's results will almost certainly be fantastic and reflect a booming underlying business. However, I have a contrarian take. I predict that the [GPU stock] will drop after Feb. 25.

Image source: Nvidia.

## A high-expectations problem

The consensus Wall Street Q4 revenue estimate for Nvidia is $65.6 billion, according to S&P Global ([SPGI] +2.18%). The average Q4 adjusted earnings estimate is $1.52 per share. Both estimates reflect year-over-year growth of roughly 71%.

Does my prediction assume that Nvidia will fail to meet these estimates? Actually, no. Nvidia has an excellent track record of beating Wall Street's numbers, delivering better-than-expected results in each of the last four quarters. I think the company will do it again.

However, beating analysts' estimates doesn't always translate to a subsequent stock gain. Look at the chart below. Each of the "Es" on the cha

news.headline: Prediction: Nvidia Stock Will Drop After Feb. 25
news.content:
# Prediction: Nvidia Stock Will Drop After Feb. 25

> Nvidia is set to announce its fiscal 2025 Q4 results on Feb. 25, 2026, with high expectations for revenue and earnings. Despite a predicted strong performance, a contrarian view suggests the stock may drop post-announcement due to potential memory shortages and skepticism around AI ROI. Historical trends show Nvidia's stock often declines after exceeding estimates. A short-term dip could present a buying opportunity for long-term investors, as the company is expected to succeed in its AI initiatives.

Many Nvidia (NVDA +1.12%) investors have probably circled Feb. 25, 2026, on their calendars. That's the date the giant chipmaker is scheduled to announce its fiscal 2025 fourth-quarter results. Nvidia plans to release its results following the market close and hold its quarterly conference call with analysts shortly afterward.

It's easy to predict that Nvidia's share price will surge after its Q4 update. The company's results will almost certainly be fantastic and reflect a booming underlying business. However, I have a contrarian take. I predict that the GPU stock will drop after Feb. 25.

Image source: Nvidia.

## A high-expectations problem

The consensus Wall Street Q4 revenue estimate for Nvidia is $65.6 billion, according to S&P Global (SPGI +0.17%). The average Q4 adjusted earnings estimate is $1.52 per share. Both estimates reflect year-over-year growth of roughly 71%.

Does my prediction assume that Nvidia will fail to meet these estimates? Actually, no. Nvidia has an excellent track record of beating Wall Street's numbers, delivering better-than-expected results in each of the last four quarters. I think the company will do it again.

agent_action.reasoning: Nvidia's current stock price is $195.62, well above the $170 threshold. Despite predictions of a potential post-earnings drop, the stock is likely to remain above $170 by the end of February. The company's strong track record of beating earnings estimates and the current price level support a high belief probability. However, since the market price is at 0.50, which is below my belief probability, I will hold my current position.
